Name: Huge Stress (⚠ 5-10 min)
Description:
WARNING: this exercise takes 5-10 minutes to drain.  It kicks 1040 operations of mixed shapes and durations across the 7 active fleet beacons (Parser, Transformer, Validator, Loader, DataIntegration, VideoTranscode, FileTransfer), deliberately producing cross-operation resource contention on every single-slot capability.  Designed to populate the queue heavily so the Ultravisor and Lab UIs have a real workload to render — Upcoming, In-Process, Stalled, and Recently-Terminal buckets all see meaningful traffic.  Useful for queue management tooling work (priority changes, reorder, cancel) and Phase 3 Observer snapshot diagnostics.

Mix (1040 ops):
  200 mixed-pipeline       (DI → Vid → File at 1.5s/stage; ~4.5s each)
  400 mixed-pipeline-fast  (DI → Vid → File at 400ms/stage; ~1.2s each)
  200 validation-only-fast (Parser → Validator at 400ms/stage; ~0.8s each)
   80 etl-linear           (P → T → V → L at 1.5s/stage; ~6s each)
   60 etl-strict           (Parser-strict → T → Validator-strict → L at 1.5s; ~6s each)
   60 bulk-load-fanout     (P → 3×T → V → L at 1.5s/stage; ~9s each)
   40 validation-only      (Parser → Validator at 1.5s; ~3s each)

Kicks are paced over ~150s so peak HTTP-connection rate stays ≤ 12/s — important on local Mac/Colima setups where the SSH-based port-forward gets overwhelmed past ~25/s.  Critical bottleneck: shared DI/Vid/File capacity at 200×1.5 + 400×0.4 = 460s ≈ 7.7 min wall.
